title: micranthes nelsoniana subsp. insularis
intro: Micranthes nelsoniana subsp. insularis, also known as Nelson's saxifrage, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the saxifrage family. It is native to the coastal regions of the Pacific Northwest in North America. The medicinal properties of Nelson's saxifrage include its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Herbal preparations of Nelson's saxifrage can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves to promote wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and support overall health and well-being.
